title: Ethereum Foundation Launches Platform Team to Strengthen L1 and L2 Integration
summary: The Ethereum Foundation has created a new Platform Team to improve the relationship between Ethereum's base layer (L1) and scaling solutions (L2s). This initiative aims to create a more cohesive ecosystem where all parts of Ethereum work together seamlessly, benefiting developers and users alike.
body:
The Ethereum Foundation (EF) has announced the formation of a new Platform Team dedicated to enhancing the integration and collaboration between Ethereum's base layer (L1) and its scaling solutions (L2s). The team's primary goal is to ensure that L1 and L2s are optimally positioned to support users, applications, and organizations building on Ethereum. This initiative is crucial for the ecosystem's growth, as it aims to foster a mutually beneficial relationship between the different layers of the Ethereum network.

The Platform Team will focus on improving the technical and operational aspects of the L1-L2 relationship. This includes developing tools and standards that facilitate better communication and interoperability between the layers. The team will also work on addressing any bottlenecks or inefficiencies that currently exist, ensuring a smoother experience for developers and end-users. By strengthening this relationship, the Ethereum Foundation hopes to create a more robust and scalable platform that can support the growing demands of the crypto community.

For everyday users and developers, this initiative means a more seamless and efficient experience when interacting with Ethereum. Improved L1-L2 integration can lead to faster transaction times, lower fees, and better overall performance. This is particularly important for developers who are building applications on Ethereum, as it will provide them with a more reliable and scalable foundation to work on. Ultimately, this could lead to more innovative and user-friendly applications being developed on the Ethereum platform.

The formation of the Platform Team is a significant step towards a more unified and efficient Ethereum ecosystem. As the team works on improving the L1-L2 relationship, users and developers can expect to see tangible benefits in the form of better performance and more seamless interactions. The Ethereum Foundation's commitment to this initiative underscores its dedication to making Ethereum the best possible platform for building decentralized applications. Keep an eye on updates from the Platform Team as they work towards these goals.
twitterThread: Ethereum's new Platform Team is here to bridge the gap between L1 and L2s, making the ecosystem stronger and more efficient.
By improving the L1-L2 relationship, the team aims to reduce bottlenecks, lower fees, and speed up transactions for all users.

This initiative could lead to a more seamless experience for developers and users alike. Read more → https://www.cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-05-14-ethereum-foundation-launches-platform-team-to-strengthen-l1-and-l2-integration
standaloneTweet: Ethereum's new Platform Team is working to make L1 and L2s work better together, which could mean faster, cheaper transactions for everyone.
